speaker: Jane
en: Hello, North Bay Aquatic Centre, Jane speaking.

speaker: Peter
en: Oh, hello.

speaker: Peter
en: I'm calling to apply for a part-time lifeguard position.

speaker: Peter
en: Am I speaking to the right person?

speaker: Jane
en: Yes, you are.

speaker: Jane
en: We are currently recruiting lifeguards for two different positions.

speaker: Jane
en: But first, I'll have to jot down some of your personal information before we officially invite you for an interview.

speaker: Jane
en: Is that alright?

speaker: Peter
en: Sure.

speaker: Peter
en: No problem.

speaker: Jane
en: May I have your name, please?

speaker: Peter
en: It's Peter, Peter Smith.

speaker: Jane
en: Okay.

speaker: Jane
en: And what is your current address?

speaker: Peter
en: It's 130 South Main Street, Lake Elsinore.

speaker: Jane
en: I'm sorry, Elsinore?

speaker: Peter
en: Oh, it's E-L-S-I-N-O-R-E, Elsinore.

speaker: Jane
en: Right.

speaker: Jane
en: Got it!

speaker: Jane
en: What is your phone number?

speaker: Peter
en: My home phone number is 0-double 7-double 9-5-3-6-2.

speaker: Peter
en: Actually, it's easier to reach me by my cellphone number.

speaker: Peter
en: I'm mainly out these days.

speaker: Jane
en: Right.

speaker: Jane
en: Can you tell me about your work experience?

speaker: Peter
en: Well, I now work as a waiter.

speaker: Peter
en: And that's just a part-time gig.

speaker: Peter
en: It requires serving customers, and I also have some cleaning duties.

speaker: Jane
en: Okay.

speaker: Jane
en: Do you have any other part-time job?

speaker: Peter
en: No.

speaker: Peter
en: But I'm majoring in physical education right now.

speaker: Peter
en: I'm learning exercise physiology, sports nutrition and individual fitness activities.

speaker: Peter
en: And I want to become a baseball coach for a local high school.

speaker: Jane
en: So, do you have any other relevant work experience to a lifeguard position?

speaker: Peter
en: Oh, right.

speaker: Peter
en: I also worked part-time as a rescue diver for a local beach last summer.

speaker: Peter
en: I'm certified for open water rescue missions.

speaker: Jane
en: Excellent.

speaker: Jane
en: That's exactly what we're looking for.

speaker: Jane
en: Before we continue, I need to know a few more things to ensure that you are qualified for maintaining water safety during work.

speaker: Peter
en: Okay.

speaker: Peter
en: Go ahead.

speaker: Jane
en: As a lifeguard, you will spend most of your time monitoring the area near the water and reducing risks to everyone here.

speaker: Jane
en: So we are looking for someone who can stay focused.

speaker: Jane
en: Do you have difficulties concentrating or any related disorders?

speaker: Peter
en: No.

speaker: Peter
en: I don't have any medical disorders relating to that sort of problem.

speaker: Jane
en: We're also expecting someone with good eyesight because a lifeguard must be able to recognise and respond to any struggling swimmers quickly.

speaker: Peter
en: I have sharp vision.

speaker: Peter
en: I mean, both eyes are 20-10 without correction.

speaker: Jane
en: Good.

speaker: Jane
en: Do you have relevant skills in terms of lifeguarding?

speaker: Peter
en: I'm good at diving.

speaker: Peter
en: You know my previous job requires training in that.

speaker: Jane
en: We only need lifeguards with certification.

speaker: Jane
en: I assume you already have one, right?

speaker: Peter
en: Yes.

speaker: Peter
en: To get the certificate, I received training for waterfront lifeguarding two years ago.

speaker: Peter
en: The training includes things like providing first aid and emergency care.

speaker: Peter
en: But mine is about to expire in November.

speaker: Peter
en: Oh, wait, let me check.

speaker: Peter
en: It's a month earlier, in October.

speaker: Jane
en: Okay.

speaker: Jane
en: You're good for now before it expires.

speaker: Jane
en: But if you want to work here for the whole year, you'll have to renew it.

speaker: Peter
en: I see.

speaker: Jane
en: What time do you prefer to work?

speaker: Peter
en: I already work part-time in a restaurant on weekdays, so I'd like to work on the weekend.

speaker: Jane
en: We already have enough people working here on Sundays.

speaker: Peter
en: I can work on Saturday mornings then.

speaker: Jane
en: Right.

speaker: Peter
en: And I can start from 6 o'clock in the morning.

speaker: Peter
en: I'm a morning person.

speaker: Jane
en: Sounds great.

speaker: Jane
en: Where did you hear about our recruitment information from?

speaker: Peter
en: I heard it on radio this morning while driving.

speaker: Peter
en: I wouldn't say like reading newspapers.

speaker: Jane
en: Thank you for calling.

speaker: Jane
en: We'll notify you about the interview details in two days.

speaker: Peter
en: Thank you.

speaker: Peter
en: Bye.

speaker: Jane
en: Goodbye.
